answer:
**'Dasta' (دستہ) and 'dastagaah' (دستگاه) do not appear as such in the Falsafa corpus's edition of Ghalib's *Diwan-E-Ghalib*.**[^1]

However, the parenthetical gloss you provide—"workshop, plant of being"—precisely evokes the opening couplet of ghazal (chapter) 101, where Ghalib uses **kaargah-e-hasti** (کارگہِ ہستی, "workshop of existence/being/life"). Here, the world is depicted as a cosmic workshop or factory (*kaargah*, a Persianate term for workshop, factory, or tent encampment-at-work) in which existence (*hasti*) produces beauty amid inevitable pain and blood—like tulips stained by the farmer's gore.[^2][^3]

> In life's vast workshop, tulips bear a stain;  
> The flash that burns our comfort is the farmer's lifeblood's rain.[^4]

> **Kaargah-e-hasti mein laala daagh-e-samaan hai**  
> **Barq-e-kharman-e-raahat khoon-e-garm-e-dehqaan hai**[^5]

This metaphor recurs thematically in the ghazal: life's "strain" as a fire self-consuming, love's neglect chaining the world, vision demanding blood, and the tulip's wild solitude dispersing sorrow like incense smoke. It underscores Ghalib's philosophical view of existence as a site of restless, bloody creation—beauty forged in suffering, not pastoral idyll.[^6]

No other Ghalib passages match as closely (e.g., "guldasta" is a mere "bouquet," "dastaar" a "turban"). If this references a letter, commentary, or variant edition outside the *Diwan*, the corpus lacks it.[^7]

[^1]: Searches for "dasta," "dastagaah," "dasta ghalib," and variants yielded no exact corpus matches in Ghalib; nearest were Iqbal or unrelated (*e.g.*, "guldasta-e-nigah"). [list_works & search_corpus results].

[^2]: *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 101, wiki card (key passage TextRank #1). [/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/101-kaargah-e-hasti-mein-laala-daagh-e-samaan-hai/wiki/101-kaargah-e-hasti-mein-laala-daagh-e-samaan-hai.card.md/]

[^3]: Work wiki (ch.101 excerpt: "life vast workshop"). [/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/wiki/_work.card.md/]

[^4]: *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 101 (*translation*), ¶ [p-8f1a56]. [/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/101-kaargah-e-hasti-mein-laala-daagh-e-samaan-hai/translation/#p-8f1a56]

[^5]: *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 101 (*transliteration*), ¶ [p-0740da]. [/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/101-kaargah-e-hasti-mein-laala-daagh-e-samaan-hai/transliteration/#p-0740da]

[^6]: Full chapter (*translation*). [/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/101-kaargah-e-hasti-mein-laala-daagh-e-samaan-hai/translation/]

[^7]: "Workshop" searches confirm ch.101 as Ghalib's sole hit; no "plant of being."
